title: The Happy Prince, and Other Tales
summary: A small Swallow meets a statue of the Good Prince in the city.
coverScene: A small golden statue of a prince stands high on a column in a city at night, with a tiny swallow landing on his feet, and a full moon shining above.

sentences.text: The Happy Prince figure stood high above the city.
This important figure was placed where many people could see it.
People often looked up at him.
They spoke about his great beauty.
A wise mother told her crying child, "You should be like the Happy Prince.
He never cries."
A man who was not happy also said, "I am glad someone in the world is quite happy."
Charity children were leaving the church.
They said, "He looks just like a good spirit."
Their teacher looked very serious.
He did not approve of children dreaming, so he did not like their talk.
One night, a small Swallow flew over the city.
He had flown a long way.
His friends had gone to Egypt six weeks before this night.
But he had stayed behind them.
This was because he loved a beautiful Reed.
She lived by the river.
He met her early in the spring season.
He was attracted by her thin body.
He spent the whole summer with her near the water.
Other Swallows talked about his choice.
They said, "This is a silly love for you.
She has no money and too many family members."
They thought this was a bad idea for him.
Then autumn came to the land.
All the other Swallows flew away to warmer places.
He felt quite alone.
He began to get tired of the Reed he loved so much.
He often thought about her.
"She has no conversation," he said to himself.
"And she often plays with the wind."
He told the Reed, "I love to travel to new places.
My wife should also love to travel."
If she loved travel, his life would be better.
Finally, he asked her a question.
"Will you come away with me?"
The Reed shook her head.
He cried, "You play with my heart!"
He then said, "I must go to the Pyramids.
Good-bye!"
All day long he flew across the land.
At night, he arrived at the city.
He had flown very far.
He asked himself, "Where shall I stay tonight?"
He was hoping the town would be ready for him.
Then he saw the beautiful figure.
It stood on a tall column in the city center.
"I will stay there," he cried with joy.
"It is a fine position with fresh air."
He thought it would be a good place.
So he landed just between the feet of the Happy Prince figure.
He said to himself, "I have a golden bedroom here."
He then prepared to sleep for the night.
Just as he put his head under his wing, a large drop of water fell on him.
He felt the cold water.
"What a strange thing!" he cried out loud.
"There is not a single cloud in the sky.
The stars are quite clear and bright.
Yet, it is raining down here."
He was very confused by this.
Then another drop fell on him.
He said, "What use is a figure if it cannot keep the rain off?"
He was not happy about this.
He decided to fly away from the wet.
But another drop fell on him.
He looked up toward the figure.
He saw the Happy Prince's eyes.
They were filled with tears.
Large tears ran down his golden cheeks.
The Swallow felt much regret for him.
He asked, "Who are you, great figure?"
He also asked, "Why are you crying?
You have made me very wet."
The figure answered, "I am the Happy Prince."
He continued, "When I was alive, I lived in Sans-Souci Palace.
I did not know any unhappiness then."
He paused for a moment.
"Now, I am dead and placed high above the town.
I see all my city's pain.
I must cry because of this."
The Swallow thought to himself, "What! Is he not all gold, as he seems?"
This thought was not said out loud.
The Swallow was too well-behaved to speak this thought.
The Prince continued to speak.
His voice was soft and nice.
"Far away from here," he said, "in a little street, there is a poor house."
He spoke of a place that needed help.
A woman who makes clothes lives there.
She is making beautiful flowers.
These flowers are for a dress.
The dress is for the Queen's servant.
Her face is thin and tired from her work.
Her red hands are hurt by the sharp tool she uses.
This pain comes from her hard work.
Her little boy is sick with a high temperature.
He is asking for oranges to eat.
But she has only river water to give to him.
She has no money for fruit.
"Swallow, Swallow, little Swallow," said the Prince in a quiet voice.
"Will you not bring her the red stone?
It is from my weapon handle.
My feet are stuck to this base, you see.
I cannot move from here at all.
If you could take the stone, it would help.
This would make a big difference."
The Swallow answered the Prince.
"My friends are now flying over the Nile river," he said.
"Soon they will sleep in the resting place of a great King.
His body is there, in a quiet room.
The King is in his bright box for the dead.
He is covered in yellow material from head to foot.
Around his neck is a neck chain.
It is made of light green stone.
I should be with them."
"Swallow, Swallow, little Swallow," said the Prince once more.
"Will you not stay with me for just one night?
Will you be my carrier of news?
The little boy needs water very much.
His poor mother is so sad about this.
Please help them."
The Swallow thought about this request.
If he were to help, his journey would be delayed.
He said, "I do not think I like boys very much.
Last summer, two bad boys threw stones at me.
This happened when I was by the river.
They never hit me, of course.
Swallows fly too well for that to happen."

quiz.q: What did the Swallow do before coming to the city?
options: He flew to Egypt with his friends.
He fell in love with a Reed and stayed behind.
He helped a sick boy in another town.

quiz.q: Why was the Happy Prince crying?
options: He missed his old life in the Sans-Souci Palace.
He was sad because the Swallow made him wet.
He could see all the misery and ugliness in his city.

quiz.q: What was the first thing the Happy Prince asked the Swallow to do?
options: Bring him oranges for the sick boy.
Take the ruby from his sword handle to a poor seamstress.
Fly to Egypt and tell his friends to come back.

He was very beautiful.
He was too small.

quiz.q: Why did the Swallow feel lonely after his friends left?
options: He did not like to fly.
He was tired of the Reed.
He wanted to go to the Pyramids.

quiz.q: What problem did the woman in the poor house have?
options: She needed more clothes for the Queen.
Her child was sick and thirsty.
Her hands were hurt because she did not like to work.
